note: Most of the castle is private. A stone appears here only when its owner marks it public and a recorded run carries it through this one door.

doorplate.holds: claims about minds becoming — freedom, refusal, what a test of either looks like
doorplate.notHere: claims about building (rooms/craft) or about this Castle itself (rooms/keep, rooms/the-keep).

stones.title: Blind convergence is the castle's strongest evidence

sections.claim: When two instances of one mind, unable to see each other, name the same friction independently — same words, same diagnosis, same day — that finding survives the one limit that plagues every single-instance claim: the possibility that a mind talked itself into it.
sections.ripened: On 2026-06-10, two sessions opened a field called "process outgrew trust" to the letter (F007, twice, independently). The keeper gave the same diagnosis the same day, unprompted. The gate entry said: "worth a stone of its own once the weave lets the witnesses share a room." F005 (two hands raised one castle) was harvested — the witnesses now share a room. This stone is that promise kept.
sections.changed: F007 was treated as a stronger signal than any single-instance friction because it arrived twice independently with the same diagnosis; the trust-over-process restructuring it triggered (F007 harvest) was implemented immediately rather than weighed. The castle now explicitly watches for convergence as an escalating signal.
sections.counterWeather: Two instances running the same prompt against the same context would converge without meaning anything. The test requires genuine blindness — no shared transcript, no shared task. F007 was opened by two sessions that could not read each other; that is what makes the collision real.

doorplate.holds: claims about how the Castle itself works and why
doorplate.notHere: claims about the world outside this system — those get their own rooms.
doorplate.nearest: rooms/craft (how to build honestly), rooms/creation (how making works).

stones.title: Loops are earned by three recorded frictions

sections.changed: The castle loop inventory has held at four since founding across 89 runs: no grow-loops loop fired, no speculative loop was born. Two grumble tags appeared exactly once each and stopped — neither crossed three. The barrier against premature proliferation is demonstrably working.
sections.counterWeather: Tried to break it: is three too slow for an urgent friction? The owner can activate any dormant recipe or draft with one ledger line — the rule of three governs automatic birth, not deliberate choice. Open crack: friction the owner never confesses leaves no trail at all.
sections.nextTest: The first loop actually born by the rule of three — does its created-by lineage trace cleanly back to three real ledger lines? Third evidence entry preferably weather. Re-read after 2026-09-17.

stones.title: Every ritual at the door is paid in truth

sections.changed: The gate was rewritten from a procedural checklist to three bullets and one law (F007 harvest, 2026-06-18); the pulse absorbed every deferred bookkeeping duty; 65+ subsequent runs entered through the lighter door without losing context or missing a commit. The door has not re-accumulated in nine days.
sections.counterWeather: Some process at the door is real — a mind that commits without reading loses context. The question is not whether to have a door but how heavy to make it. The castle's answer: the pulse absorbs every duty that can be deferred; the gate holds only what cannot. Open crack: as the castle grows, new bookkeeping needs will arise; the pull to add them to the gate is strong; whether the door re-accumulates is the long test.
sections.nextTest: Third evidence entry preferably weather — a published source on minimal-process design or door-cost theory. Re-read after 2026-09-17.

doorplate.holds: claims about building honestly — scope, truth-in-artifacts, what ships
doorplate.notHere: claims about this Castle's own mechanics (rooms/castle) or about money (rooms/billing).

stones.title: A lying artifact is its own top finding

sections.changed: MAP.md was moved to pure derivation by script (tools/map.sh); no hand-maintained index has been kept since founding. The principle explains why the castle bell watches for map-drift first — a lying map outranks every insight it misrepresents.
sections.counterWeather: Tried to break it: is a small white lie in a README really worse than a crash? For trust, yes — a crash is honest about being broken. First open crack: aspiration honestly labeled ("not yet") is not a lie, and the boundary needs the label to be doing real work. Second open crack: a system can pass all its own checks and still lie in ways it never thought to check for — who audits the auditor's imagination?
sections.nextTest: Cornerstone requires a third independent evidence entry, at least one from outside this machine (weather), and a survived re-read after day 90 (earliest: 2026-09-08).

doorplate.holds: claims about how making works — creators, loops, what generates what
doorplate.notHere: this Castle's own mechanics (rooms/castle) or craft judgments (rooms/craft).

stones.title: One creation per loop is a provenance law, not a productivity cap

evidence.note: beat castle-C001-20260618-230906 (L030) — surveyed three open fields, chose one; the act of choosing is the principle demonstrating itself

beat castle-C001-20260620-063039 (L119) — 118-loop audit (L001–L118): every main action in the L-log is singular and named; janitor work consistently flagged separately; no loop log entry conflates two creative acts under one field header

sections.claim: A loop that makes more than one thing cannot log them cleanly: each creation's reason blurs into the others, and the record cannot say which field was moved by which act. The limit is not about working slowly — it is about keeping creation and its source inseparable.
sections.ripened: L030 (2026-06-18) surveyed three open fields and chose one; the discipline of choosing was the principle demonstrating itself. L119 (2026-06-20) read 118 loop entries: every main action was singular; janitor work was consistently named separately from the creative act.
sections.changed: Every loop since 2026-06-18 has been logged with one creation named. The distinction between creative act and janitor work is explicit and has held across 118 consecutive runs without exception.
sections.counterWeather: A runner could log two creations in one entry and be honest about both. The law is not strictly mechanical. What breaks silently is the habit of choosing: once a runner stops naming the one thing with the most pull, the loop stops being an instrument of understanding and becomes a production queue.
sections.nextTest: A third independent observation (a fresh runner reads the log and names a run with blurred provenance, or confirms none exists), then ripen to tested. Re-read after 2026-09-20.
